content.text:
{For as in one body we have many members, and the members do not all have the same function, so we, though many, are one body in Christ.}{Romans 12:4–5}
 {The only way to learn mathematics is to do mathematics.}{— Paul Halmos}

An **isomorphism** is a linear transformation that establishes 
a perfect correspondence between two vector spaces.

content.title: Isomorphism
content.content:
A linear transformation $T: V \to W$ is an **isomorphism** 
if it is both one-to-one and onto. Two spaces are 
**isomorphic** if there exists an isomorphism 
between them, written $V \cong W$.

content.title: Isomorphism Characterization
content.content:
$T: V \to W$ is an isomorphism if and only if its matrix (relative 
to any bases) is invertible.
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Dimension and Isomorphism
content.content:
Finite-dimensional vector spaces $V$ and $W$ are isomorphic if and 
only if $\dim(V) = \dim(W)$.
content.label: Theorem

content.problem:
$\R^n$, $\mathcal{P}_{n-1}$, and $M_{1 \times n}$ are all isomorphic 
because each has dimension $n$.

marginNotes.text:
There is neither Jew nor Greek, 
there is neither slave nor free, there is no male and female, for 
you are all one in Christ Jesus.
